generated_body:
Subject: Correct Is Not Usable — arXiv plan + two asks

Hey Idan,

Two asks on the Correct Is Not Usable paper before it goes out:

1. **arXiv endorsement:** cs.CL requires a prior author to endorse first-time submitters. Do you have standing there? If yes, would you be down to endorse mine. If not, no worries — I'll route to one of the cited eval-paper authors.

2. **Co-author byline:** the experiment grew out of Zerg eval infra, so you're the natural co-author candidate. Genuinely your call, either way is fine, just want to know before it ships.

Quick recap in case it's been a minute:
- 21 seed tasks × 5 prompt conditions × 4 models (gpt-5-mini, gpt-5-nano, gemini-2.5-flash, claude-haiku-4-5), 420 rows balanced
- 396/420 semantically correct (94%), 181/420 strict first-pass usable (43%)
- Acceptance-contract condition lifts strict usability +52.4pp (95% CI [+41.6, +63.1]) on the balanced 4-model run, +59.5pp on the OpenAI two-model pilot
- Length-matched generic control rules out length as the variable (5/28 vs 26/28)
- Plan: paired Zerg blog (primary distribution) + arXiv preprint (citable handle), skipping peer review for v1

Draft + companion + 60-row reviewer bundle at `MattZerg/Research/Experiments/Correct-Is-Not-Usable/paper/draft.md`, `paper/publication-target.md`, and `Writing/correct-is-not-usable.md`.

Soft deadline: reviewer return ~5/19, so the byline and endorsement path can resolve in parallel.

Let me know!

Matt
verdict: dropped: internal one-off (arXiv ask), no voice pattern

Hi Real Thread team,

Hope all is well! I'm Matt, head of growth at Zerg (zergai.com) — an
early-stage AI startup. We're putting together our first internal team
swag run and shortlisted Real Thread based on your soft tri-blend
reputation.

Here's what we're looking at:

   - - T-shirts: ~80 units
   - - Hats: ~40 units
   - - Hoodies: ~20 units


Quantities are a bit flexible — if there's a meaningful price break at a
higher tier, happy to hear about it.

On design:

   - Production-ready (or very close to it), single-color or small-color
   - logo
   - Straightforward main placement
   - Interested in an alt logo on the hat side panel, t-shirt back, and
   hoodie sleeve (or similar) — ideally embroidered if possible


Premium feel matters more than rock-bottom price for us.

Would appreciate a quote covering:

   - Garment recommendations (your best soft-feel tee, hoodie blank, and
   - hat for embroidery)
   - Per-unit pricing at those quantities, plus any volume-break tiers
   - Turnaround time
   - Sample/proof policy


Happy to send art files once we've confirmed a good fit. Let me know if you
need anything else from me to put together a quote.

Best,

Matthew
verdict: dropped: FM draft was CRM-error meta-note, no real prior attempt

generated_body:
Hi Vicky,

Thanks - standard waterproof vinyl works for us. Art files attached: three designs (the Zerg wordmark, a terminal graphic called dangerously-skip-permissions, and 'plz fix'), each ~3" on the longest side, split roughly even across ~1000 total. The wordmark and plz fix are single-color with black and white variants - pick whichever reads better on the stock. The terminal graphic is full-color (6 colors).

Could you recommend the shipping option that best balances cost and speed to the US, and let me know what holographic would add per piece on one design? I'd like to start with a sample run.

Best,

Matthew
